notes: Uni already has strong US distribution and brand recognition. Amazon shows extensive reviews and established market. Hard to differentiate.
Midori already available in US via specialty retailers. Leuchtturm1917 and Moleskine dominate premium notebook space. Crowded market.
Tombow has US presence. Shake-advance is novelty feature not compelling enough for new brand. Market too small.
Erasers are commodity items with razor-thin margins. Hard to build brand premium. Low price point ($2-3 retail) makes DTC difficult.
Pilot FriXion already hugely successful in US market with strong brand recognition and distribution. Cannot compete.
Dividers/tabs are low-value commodity items. Avery dominates US market at low prices. Hard to command premium.
Ceramic polymer formula likely proprietary/patented. Cannot easily replicate. Would need chemistry expertise and significant R&D.
Muji has US stores and online presence. Ultra-fine pens available from multiple brands. Not differentiated enough.
Pilot has strong US distribution. Many gel pen brands offer wide color selection. Market crowded.
Precision scissors available from Fiskars and other US brands. Low price point, commodity market. Hard to differentiate.
Unique tip shape likely requires custom mold/tooling. Higher capital requirement. Incremental improvement over standard highlighters not compelling enough.
3M Post-it is dominant US brand. Combo flag-highlighter products already exist from multiple brands. Cannot compete with 3M.
Uni has US presence. Dr. Grip and other ergonomic pencils/pens well-established. Gel grip not unique enough differentiator.
Zebra Sarasa available in US. Binder clip is nice feature but not compelling enough for standalone brand. Gel pen market very crowded.
Sakura Pigma Micron is THE standard for archival pens in US - sold in every art store. Extremely well-established. Cannot compete.
